headlines.title: Kim Jong-un Oversees Test of 2,500-Kilonewton Engine for North Korea ICBMs
headlines.summary: North Korea tested a high-thrust solid-fuel engine producing 2,500 kilonewtons, up from prior tests, capable of powering ICBMs to strike global targets including the U.S. mainland. Kim Jong-un hailed it as boosting strategic power. Analysts see potential for multiple warheads, heightening global tensions amid Iran war.

framings.angle: Emphasized thrust increase to 2,500 kilonewtons and potential ICBM launch
Highlighted engine for missiles striking U.S. mainland with multiple warheads
Linked test to Kim's accusations of U.S. terrorism amid Iran conflict

headlines.title: Israeli Strike Kills Three Journalists in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Israeli military strike in southern Lebanon kills three journalists, including one from Hezbollah-linked Al Manar TV whom Israel accuses of operative ties. Lebanon condemns the attack. Incident escalates media safety concerns and regional conflict risks.

framings.angle: Focused on Israeli accusation of Hezbollah ties and Lebanese president's condemnation
Confirmed Israeli military admission of killing Hezbollah-affiliated reporter

headlines.title: Iranian Guards Strike Aluminum Plants in Bahrain and UAE
headlines.summary: Iranian Revolutionary Guards target aluminum facilities in Bahrain and UAE, wounding two employees at Aluminium Bahrain, a top global producer. Strikes heighten Gulf tensions amid Iran war. Affected workers receive treatment; regional energy and industry face disruptions.

headlines.title: U.S. Marines Arrive in Middle East as Houthis Enter Regional War
headlines.summary: U.S. Marines deploy to Middle East amid Houthi missile attacks on Israel and updated reports of Iranian strike injuring two dozen U.S. troops in Saudi Arabia. Deployment bolsters defenses. Escalation threatens broader conflict and global shipping lanes.

headlines.title: Drone Strikes Kill 28 Civilians in Sudan North Darfur and Kordofan
headlines.summary: Two drone strikes hit North Darfur market killing 22 including infant and North Kordofan truck killing 6, injuring 17 total as civil war nears fourth year. Army and RSF blamed. Civilian deaths underscore humanitarian crisis affecting millions.

headlines.title: NASA Presents Artemis 2 Crew for First Moon Orbit Mission in 52 Years
headlines.summary: NASA introduces diverse Artemis 2 crew including commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, and specialists Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen for 2026 lunar orbit. Australia provides comms support. Mission revives U.S. moon program, advances deep space exploration.

framings.angle: Profiled crew diversity contrasting Apollo era white male pilots
Highlighted Canberra comms role in data relay for lunar orbit

headlines.title: Ukraine Finalizes Air Defense Deals with Qatar and Saudi Arabia
headlines.summary: Ukraine president secures deals to supply air defenses to Gulf nations Qatar and Saudi Arabia during Middle East visit. Shifts Kyiv to arms exporter amid Mideast war. Boosts Ukraine revenue and Gulf security against regional threats.

headlines.title: Senate Homeland Security Funding Deal Unravels Before U.S. Shutdown Deadline
headlines.summary: Senate agreement to fund Department of Homeland Security collapses Friday, splitting Republicans and risking longest U.S. partial shutdown. Affects border security and disaster response. Congress faces urgent renegotiation amid budget battles.

headlines.title: NATO Coordinates Response After Another Baltic Sea Cable Severance
headlines.summary: NATO launches first coordinated action following suspected sabotage severing another deep-sea cable in Baltic Sea. Targets critical infrastructure. Heightens alliance vigilance against hybrid threats from Russia amid regional tensions.
headlines.previousContext: Another cable severed prompting NATO's initial coordinated infrastructure defense measures
